name: env-config
description: Environment configuration and secrets management skill using UV for Python projects. Handles .env files, environment variables, secrets encryption, multi-environment setups, and secure configuration patterns. Use when setting up project environments, managing API keys, or implementing configuration best practices.
capabilities: Parse and validate .env files with required variables
Check for security issues like placeholder values and short secrets
Encrypt and decrypt secrets files using Fernet encryption
Generate .env templates from existing configuration files
Compare environment files between development and production
author: AutumnsGrove
securitySummary: This is a legitimate environment configuration management skill. The 984 static findings are predominantly false positives triggered by documentation patterns. The skill's core functionality involves reading .env files (expected behavior), using standard cryptography libraries securely, and providing installation examples from official sources. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unauthorized access patterns.
valueStatement: Managing environment configurations across multiple environments is error-prone and risky. This skill provides utilities for parsing .env files, validating required variables, encrypting secrets, and comparing configurations between environments.
limitations: Requires manual password entry for encryption/decryption
Does not integrate with external secret managers like Vault

useCases.title: Set up project configuration
useCases.description: Create secure .env files and templates for new Python projects using UV package manager.

useCases.title: Compare environments
useCases.description: Validate that development and production environments have matching required configuration.

useCases.title: Encrypt sensitive config
useCases.description: Protect secrets.json files with encryption for safer deployment and storage.

promptTemplates.title: Create env template
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a .env.template file for my Python project with UV. I need database URL, API keys for Anthropic and OpenAI, and JWT secret configuration.

promptTemplates.title: Validate configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Validate my .env.production file has all required variables: DATABASE_URL, SECRET_KEY, and API keys. Report any missing values.

promptTemplates.title: Encrypt secrets
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an encrypted version of my secrets.json file. Use a secure password and show how to decrypt it later.

promptTemplates.title: Compare environments
promptTemplates.prompt: Compare my .env.development and .env.production files. List all variables that exist in one but not the other, and any that have different values.

bestPractices: Always add .env and secrets.json files to .gitignore before committing
Use different configuration files for each environment (development, staging, production)
Generate .env.template files without actual secrets to share with team members
antiPatterns: Never commit actual API keys, passwords, or secrets to version control
Avoid using placeholder values like 'password123' or 'changeme' in production
Do not use the same secrets across development and production environments

faq.question: What is the difference between .env and .env.template?
faq.answer: .env contains actual secrets (added to .gitignore). .env.template shows structure with placeholders (safe to commit).

faq.question: How does encryption work in env_helper.py?
faq.answer: Uses Fernet symmetric encryption with PBKDF2-SHA256 key derivation. Password required for both encryption and decryption.

faq.question: Can I use this with Poetry or pip instead of UV?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill focuses on UV but concepts apply to any Python project. Replace uv add with pip install for dependencies.

faq.question: How do I validate required variables?
faq.answer: Use: python scripts/env_helper.py validate .env --required APP_NAME DATABASE_URL SECRET_KEY

faq.question: Should I use JSON or .env for secrets?
faq.answer: .env is simpler for most cases. JSON works better for complex nested structures or when integrating with certain frameworks.

faq.question: How do I mask values in generated templates?
faq.answer: The generate_env_template function automatically masks values with 'KEY', 'SECRET', 'PASSWORD', or 'TOKEN' in the variable name.
